fitness_functions.title: claude-code-hook-observability
fitness_functions.rule: Every PostToolUse/PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit hook in `.claude/settings.json` must (1) tee stderr to `.claude/logs/<hook_event>.log`, (2) propagate the underlying tool's exit code rather than masking it with `exit 0`, (3) emit a structured event line per invocation with timestamp + matcher + file_path (when applicable) + tool_exit_code + tool_version.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: Any hook command in .claude/settings.json contains `2>/dev/null` OR `exit 0` after a write-capable command OR omits a tee to .claude/logs/ OR omits a structured event-line emit
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: vgh-specific (only this repo has Claude Code hooks); pattern applies family-wide if other repos add hooks
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Lint via simple regex on .claude/settings.json hooks[].command strings. Static check; no runtime instrumentation needed. Enforcement tech: jq + grep in a CI step. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: claude-code-hook-scope-declaration
fitness_functions.rule: Every PostToolUse hook must declare a typed `filesystem_scope` (paths it is allowed to modify) and `approval_mode` (auto-approve|ask|deny) explicitly in a hook-config schema, even when matching `Write|Edit` broadly.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: A hook in .claude/settings.json has a matcher that triggers on Write|Edit but does not declare filesystem_scope OR approval_mode fields
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: vgh-specific; pattern applies family-wide if other repos add hooks
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Requires extending Claude Code's hook schema OR adopting a parallel manifest at `.claude/hooks-manifest.json`. Enforcement: schema validator in CI. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: pin-github-actions-to-sha
fitness_functions.rule: Every `uses:` reference in `.github/workflows/*.yml` must pin to a 40-hex SHA, not to a major-version tag (`@v4`) or branch.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: Any `uses:` value matches `@v[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*(\.[0-9]+)?$` or `@<branch>` instead of `@<40-hex>`
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Cross-repo applicable to any sibling adding Actions later (planning-summer-2026 currently markdown-only; new-direction-2026 has no workflows; meta-inventory has none)
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Trivial grep with negative-pattern match: `grep -nE 'uses:.*@v[0-9]' .github/workflows/*.yml && exit 1`. Tools: dependabot can auto-update SHA pins, or use a dedicated action like `step-security/secure-repo` pin-check. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: per-job-actions-permissions
fitness_functions.rule: GitHub Actions workflow files must declare `permissions:` at job level, not workflow level. Workflow-root permissions block must be either absent or restricted to `contents: read` only.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: Workflow-root `permissions:` block contains any write permission (pages:write, id-token:write, contents:write, etc.) OR any job lacks its own per-job `permissions:` block when running with elevated capabilities
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Cross-repo applicable to any sibling adding Actions; same enforcement mechanism as FF-003
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Static YAML parser check. Tools: yq + grep, or actionlint with custom rule. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: maat-positioning-narrative-balance
fitness_functions.rule: Operator maintains a CURRENT-positioning list (e.g., in AGENTS.md or a frontmatter file). Cross-check: no project name appears in `<title>` + favicon + lead-paragraph + dedicated-page + nav-card simultaneously unless it is on the current-positioning list.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: A project name appears on N≥3 surface kinds while NOT being on the operator-declared current-positioning list (operator-declared list does not exist at HEAD `ee6c7e5`, so FF starts as manual-review-only)
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Pair B only with new-direction-2026 FF-005 (same pattern). Mirror at sibling producer side.
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Cannot be automated without an operator-declared current-positioning contract. Phase 10.5 will demote to `manual-review-only` if no contract exists. Phase 10 names the rule as a future safeguard awaiting prerequisite. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: html-source-revision-provenance-comment
fitness_functions.rule: Every generated HTML page in `dist/` must embed a `<!-- source: <sibling-repo> @ <sha> | generated: <timestamp> -->` comment in the `<head>` referencing the upstream content-source repository commit SHA.
fitness_functions.scope: dist/**/*.html + build pipeline (vite.config.js or post-build script)
fitness_functions.failure_condition: A built HTML page in `dist/` lacks the source-comment OR the SHA does not match a real commit in the sibling repo OR the timestamp is more than 30 days old
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Pair A consumer-side; pairs with sibling new-direction-2026 FF-006 producer-side check
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Vite plugin (transform/transformIndexHtml hook) reads upstream sibling's commit SHA via `git -C ../new-direction-2026 rev-parse HEAD` and injects the comment. CI grep validates presence. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: docs-cross-reference-check
fitness_functions.rule: Every file/path reference in governance docs (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, DEV_GUIDE.md, THEMES.md, README.md, .cursor/rules/*.mdc, SKILL.md) must point to a file that exists at HEAD, OR be explicitly marked `(historical reference)`.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: A path-like string (matching `[a-z][a-z0-9/_.-]+\.(html|css|js|md|json|yml|svg)`) in any listed file does not resolve to a real file at HEAD AND is not annotated as historical
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: vgh-novel; could apply family-wide (planning-summer-2026 has tools/lint_docs.py; this is a sister check)
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Python script extracts path-shaped strings, checks filesystem existence. Tools: ripgrep + Python pathlib. Add to mise `ci` task. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: branch-protection-declaration
fitness_functions.rule: Required CI gates (quality, build) must be declared as required-for-merge in a checked-in `.github/branch-protection.json` (or equivalent IaC), even if the actual enforcement is at GitHub.com.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: AGENTS.md or `.github/branch-protection.json` does not enumerate the required CI gates with a `required: true` flag
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Cross-repo applicable; cross-cites sibling planning-summer-2026 FF-008 (same enforcement-class problem)
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: manual-review-only by enforcement nature — GitHub branch-protection state can be exported via `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/branches/main/protection` to a JSON file in a scheduled workflow, but the comparison requires admin token. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: biome-rule-severity-progression
fitness_functions.rule: Every biome rule severity must be `error`, OR have an inline justification comment (`// FF-009: warn-temporarily because <reason> until <date|sha>`) when set to `warn`.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: Any rule in biome.json `rules.*` is set to `warn` without an adjacent comment matching the justification pattern; rules set to `info` or `off` fail same check
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Trivial CI grep on biome.json. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: copyright-year-check
fitness_functions.rule: README.md and any LICENSE/COPYRIGHT files must declare a copyright year-range that includes the current calendar year.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: Any matched copyright string (e.g., `© <year>` or `<year> Jeffrey Johnson`) has a year less than current year - 0 (i.e., must include the current year)
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Single-line grep with `date +%Y`. Add to mise `ci` task. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: mcp-project-scoping
fitness_functions.rule: Every MCP server referenced by name in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any SKILL.md must have a corresponding entry in `.mcp.json` (project-scoped) OR be explicitly annotated `(operator-installed; not project-scoped; see <URL>)`.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: An MCP server name (e.g., `context7`, `memory`, `brave-search`, `firecrawl`, `github`) appears in any listed file without a matching `.mcp.json` entry AND without the operator-installed annotation
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Family-wide; cross-cites sibling new-direction-2026 FF-007 (mcp-json-presence) — same pattern, this version is more lenient (allows operator-installed annotation)
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Python script: extract MCP names from CLAUDE.md MCP table, cross-check .mcp.json + operator-installed annotations. Owner: jeffrey.

fitness_functions.title: mcp-tool-schema-recorded
fitness_functions.rule: Every MCP tool referenced in instruction surfaces (CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, .cursor/rules/) must have a schema record either in `.mcp.json` OR in a separate `.mcp-schemas/<server>/<tool>.json` file with at minimum a tool name, description, and input-parameter shape.
fitness_functions.failure_condition: An MCP tool name (e.g., `mcp__context7__resolve-library-id`, `mcp__memory__search_nodes`) appears in any instruction surface without a corresponding schema record
fitness_functions.cross_repo_applicability: Family-wide; complements FF-011; cross-cites sibling AAF-NDM-008
fitness_functions.implementation_notes: Tool schemas may be retrieved at runtime from each MCP server's `tools/list` and snapshotted to `.mcp-schemas/`. Owner: jeffrey.

cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-003 pin-github-actions-to-sha: Family-wide applicable; this repo first to surface
cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-004 per-job-actions-permissions: Family-wide applicable; this repo first to surface
cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-005 maat-positioning-narrative-balanc: Pair B only; mirrors sibling new-direction-2026 FF-005
cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-006 html-source-revision-provenance-c: Pair A consumer-side; pairs with sibling new-direction-2026 FF-006 producer-side
cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-007 docs-cross-reference-check: vgh-novel; could extend to family-wide (planning-summer-2026 has tools/lint_docs.py)
cross_repo_ff_alignment.FF-011 mcp-project-scoping: Family-wide; cross-cites sibling new-direction-2026 FF-007 + AAF-NDM-008
cross_repo_ff_alignment.Cross-cites planning FF-012 + sibling ND: Not duplicated here because vgh already has .claude/settings.json — the cross-repo rule is satisfied on this side; planning + new-direction need the cross-repo FF more

phase_10_exit_check.enforcement_tech_exists_or_demoted_to_ma: FF-005 + FF-008 are manual-review-only by enforcement nature; FF-001..FF-004, FF-006, FF-007, FF-009..FF-012 all have concrete CI-implementable mechanisms
